---
title: "index"
author: "Wells"
date: "2026-02-25"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(stringr)
library(readr)
library(purrr)

```

```{r}
# Replace "ProQuestDocuments.txt" with the actual path to your file
file_content <- read_file("/Users/gizmofo/Downloads/Afro Cheltenham articles/ProQuestDocuments-2025-11-19.txt")
```



```{r}
transform_to_csv <- function(raw_text) {
  # 1. Remove tags (Equivalent to the Python re.sub)
  # Note: Adjust the regex pattern if needed based on the specific tags
  clean_text <- str_replace_all(raw_text, "\\\\", "")
  
  # 2. Split the document by the separator line
  separator <- "____________________________________________________________"
  blocks <- str_split(clean_text, fixed(separator))[[1]]
  
  records <- map(blocks, function(block) {
    # Strip whitespace and split into lines
    lines <- str_split(block, "\n")[[1]] %>%
      str_trim() %>%
      .[. != ""] # Remove empty lines
    
    if (length(lines) < 2) {
      return(NULL)
    }
    
    # The first two lines are always title and url
    record <- list(
      title = lines[1],
      url = lines[2]
    )
    
    current_field <- NULL
    
    # 3. Process remaining lines to find fields (Key: Value)
    if (length(lines) > 2) {
      for (line in lines[3:length(lines)]) {
        # Regex to look for "Field Name: Value"
        match <- str_match(line, "^([^:]+):\\s*(.*)")
        
        if (!is.na(match[1, 1])) {
          field_name <- str_trim(match[1, 2])
          field_value <- str_trim(match[1, 3])
          
          record[[field_name]] <- field_value
          current_field <- field_name
        } else if (!is.null(current_field)) {
          # If no colon is found, append to the previous field (multi-line values)
          record[[current_field]] <- paste(record[[current_field]], line)
        }
      }
    }
    return(record)
  })
  
  # Remove NULL entries from the list
  records <- compact(records)
  
  # 4. Convert list of records to a single data frame
  # dplyr::bind_rows handles missing fields by filling them with NA automatically
  df <- dplyr::bind_rows(records)
  
  # Ensure "title" and "url" are the first columns, followed by others sorted alphabetically
  other_cols <- sort(setdiff(names(df), c("title", "url")))
  df <- df[, c("title", "url", other_cols)]
  
  # 5. Generate CSV content as a string
  output_csv <- format_csv(df)
  
  return(output_csv)
}

# Usage:
# csv_data <- transform_to_csv(file_content)
# cat(csv_data)
```

```{r}
# This uses the function from the previous message
csv_output <- transform_to_csv(file_content)

# To this:
write_file(csv_output, "afro_articles.csv")
```


```{r}


library(googlesheets4)
library(janitor)

gs4_deauth()
old_afro <- read_sheet("https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/11QpEVpbe9bUi1nye3t1DfyhkTWCdxbWiRDR1R85usXc/edit?usp=sharing", sheet = "Afro_fall") |>
     clean_names()

new_afro <- read_sheet("https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/11QpEVpbe9bUi1nye3t1DfyhkTWCdxbWiRDR1R85usXc/edit?usp=sharing", sheet = "afro_index") |>
     clean_names() |> 
  mutate(store_id = as.character(pro_quest_document_id))

```

```{r}
#anti_join

outliers <- old_afro |> 
  anti_join(new_afro, by = c("store_id"))



# 1. Rename the ID so they match
# 2. Only keep columns that exist in both tables
new_afro_aligned <- new_afro |> 
  select(any_of(names(old_afro)))

new_afro_deduplicated <- new_afro_aligned |> 
  # Put rows with an abstract at the top
  arrange(store_id, is.na(abstract)) |> 
  # Keep only the top row for each ID
  distinct(store_id, .keep_all = TRUE)

# Now the upsert will work because the columns match perfectly
keepers <- old_afro |> 
  rows_upsert(new_afro_deduplicated, by = "store_id")

keepers <- keepers |> 
  mutate(date = mdy(entry_date)) |> 
  arrange(date, store_id, title, reporter, year, abstract, authors, document_type, pubtitle, document_url)

keepers <- keepers |> 
  select(date, store_id, title, reporter, year, abstract, authors, document_type, pubtitle, document_url)

write_csv(keepers, "/Users/gizmofo/Library/CloudStorage/GoogleDrive-robwells@umd.edu/My Drive/Cheltenham Spring 2026 Class/Sp2026 Class Projects Cheltenham/Fall_2025_Class_Docs_Cheltenham/Afro_master/afro_index_feb_24.csv")
```
